DECLARATION (REDACTED) OF JUDITH CHOW IN OPPOS TO DEFTS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION FILED BY PLAINTIFF CHOW, JUDITH WILLIAMS, SCOTT

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS THE STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA, ROBERT HAWLEY, ELYSE CONTANT, STARR BABCOCK AND PEGGY VAN HORN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILS TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF IN THAT DEFENDANT HAS NOT DEMONSTRATED THAT PLAINTIFF IS UNABLE TO PROVE HER CLAIM OF RETALIATION. PLAINTIFF IS COMPLAINING ABOUT MULTIPLE ACTS OF RETALIATION AND THE COMPLAINT AT PARAGRAPH THIRTY-TWO IS SUFFICIENT TO PLACE DEFENDANT ON NOTICE OF THIS CLAIM AS WAS THE DEPOSITION TESTIMONY OF PLAINTIFF. UNDER MCRAE V DEPARTMENT OF CORRECTIONS & REHABILITATION (2006) 142 CAL APP 4TH 377, 387-388, A SERIES OF EMPLOYMENT ACTIONS TAKEN TOGETHER MAY BE SUFFICIENT FOR PLAINTIFF TO PREVAIL. BY LINKING THESE ACTS TO INCREASED WORKLOADS, DOWNWARD REVIEWS AND THE FAILURE TO OBTAIN A PROMOTION, PLAINTIFF MAY BE ABLE TO ESTABLISH A CAUSAL LINK BETWEEN PROTECTED ACTIVITY AND AN ADVERSE EMPLOYMENT ACTION. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST TO DISMISS THE INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANTS IS GRANTED. OBJECTION TO BENAY DECLARATION IS SUSTAINED BASED ON AN INABILITY TO LAY THE FOUNDATION FOR THE ADMISSIBILITY OF THE DOCUMENTS. OBJECTIONS TO THE CHOW DECLARATION A AND C THROUGH K ARE OVERRULED FOR PURPOSE OF PROVING ONLY THAT PLAINTIFF WAS COMPLAINING ABOUT THESE MATTERS. PARAGRAPH SEVEN ADMITTED FOR PLAINTIFF'S STATE OF MIND. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

Notice Of Motiona Ndotion To Compel Responses To Pltfs' Requests For Production Of Documents From Deft State Bar Of California
Off Calendar Apr-03-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCE:
Edith J. Benay, Esq., for plaintiff and moving party: Judith Chow and Scott Williams
Stephanie B. Bradshaw, Esq., for defendant and opposing party: The State Bar of California

AFTER HEARING, TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff Judith Chow and Scott Williams' motion to compel production of documents is denied for failure to comply with California Rule of Court 3.1020, Code of Civil Procedure Section 2024.020, and Code of Civil Procedure Section 1005. The Court finds that the motion was filed without regard to specified procedures and without substantial justification. Counsel for plaintiff shall pay monetary sanctions in the amount of $1,325 within 30 days of notice of this order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN. Not reported.

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-21-2008 Off Calendar

Ntc And Motion For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Mar-14-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS THE STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA, ROBERT HAWLEY, ELYSE CONTANT, STARR BABCOCK AND PEGGY VAN HORN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.
APPEARANCES: EDITH J. BENAY, ESQUIRE (415.241.7286) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. THERE ARE NO OTHER APPEARANCES.
RULING: COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILS TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF IN THAT DEFENDANT HAS NOT DEMONSTRATED THAT PLAINTIFF IS UNABLE TO PROVE HER CLAIM OF RETALIATION. PLAINTIFF IS COMPLAINING ABOUT MULTIPLE ACTS OF RETALIATION AND THE COMPLAINT AT PARAGRAPH THIRTY-TWO IS SUFFICIENT TO PLACE DEFENDANT ON NOTICE OF THIS CLAIM AS WAS THE DEPOSITION TESTIMONY OF PLAINTIFF. UNDER MCRAE V DEPARTMENT OF CORRECTIONS & REHABILITATION (2006) 142 CAL APP 4TH 377, 387-388, A SERIES OF EMPLOYMENT ACTIONS TAKEN TOGETHER MAY BE SUFFICIENT FOR PLAINTIFF TO PREVAIL. BY LINKING THESE ACTS TO INCREASED WORKLOADS, DOWNWARD REVIEWS AND THE FAILURE TO OBTAIN A PROMOTION, PLAINTIFF MAY BE ABLE TO ESTABLISH A CAUSAL LINK BETWEEN PROTECTED ACTIVITY AND AN ADVERSE EMPLOYMENT ACTION. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST TO DISMISS THE INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANTS IS GRANTED. OBJECTION TO BENAY DECLARATION IS SUSTAINED BASED ON AN INABILITY TO LAY THE FOUNDATION FOR THE ADMISSIBILITY OF THE DOCUMENTS. OBJECTIONS TO THE CHOW DECLARATION A AND C THROUGH K ARE OVERRULED FOR PURPOSE OF PROVING ONLY THAT PLAINTIFF WAS COMPLAINING ABOUT THESE MATTERS. PARAGRAPH SEVEN ADMITTED FOR PLAINTIFF'S STATE OF MIND. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (302/PJM/AY) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
